learning_outcomes:
Medicine (MBChB) 1. MBChB Programme: The educational objective of the
curriculum is to ensure that our students obtain the essential knowledge,
skills and attitudes for professional medical practice and life-long pursuit
of medical education. Our graduates will have acquired the ability to:
(1)  |  deliver effective and holistic
care to their patients;
(2)  |  communicate effectively,
compassionately and with good attitude with their patients;
(3)  |  make decisions rationally and
ethically when faced with conflicting choices in the management of patients;
(4)  |  critically appraise available
evidence so that they can apply the best treatment to patients;
(5)  |  have the skills for life-long
learning to equip themselves with the new knowledge required for the
provision of the best possible care to their patients.
study_scheme:
Medicine (MBChB)Medicine Programme
(MBChB)(Applicable to students admitted in 2023-24) The Medicine Programme is a six-year undergraduate professional
programme that aims to prepare students for medical internship and life-long
career as medical practitioners. Medical students have to complete the
prescribed course of studies and examinations each year before they can be
promoted to the following year. In Medicine Year One, students are required to complete the 9-unit
Faculty Package and other major courses which cover the aspects of anatomy, structure
and function of cells and tissues, bioethics, communication skills and perspectives
of medicine in real life. The Faculty Package includes 4 courses, namely
Foundation Course for Health Sciences I (for MBChB only), Foundation Course
for Health Sciences II, Public Health and Interprofessional Healthcare.
Students have to fulfill the prescribed course requirements and examinations
before promotion to Medicine Year Two. In Medicine Years Two and Three, students learn a wide range of biomedical
knowledge and its clinical application. In these two years, the focus is on human
structure, human function, molecular medicine and genetics, communication
between doctor and patient, bioethics and resilience building. A bridging
course which covers clinical pathology and clinical skills is provided before
further clinical training in Medicine Year Four. By the end of Medicine Year Three, students are required to pass
the First Professional Examination before they can be promoted to Medicine
Year Four. In Medicine Year Four, students are required to complete studies
in various courses including Junior Medical Clerkship, Junior Surgical Dressership,
Skills Module, Doctor and Patient, Anatomical Pathology and Clinical
Hematology, Chemical Pathology, Clinical Microbiology, Radiology Pathology, Integrated
Clinical Communication Skills, bioethics and resilience building. Medical
students have to pass the Second Professional Examination held in Medicine
Year Four before promotion to Medicine Year Five. In Medicine Year Five, students have clinical attachments to four
clinical disciplines namely, obstetrics and gynaecology, paediatrics, psychiatry,
and community and family medicine. They are also required to complete
studies in Integrated Clinical Communication Skills and bioethics. By the
end of the year, they sit for Part I of the Third Professional Examination in
four modules and the Combined Clinical Examination. Students take their
Clinical Elective at the end of Medicine Year Five. In Medicine Year Six, students attach to the clinical teams
through rotations through medicine, surgery and a range of specialties. They
assist interns in the care of patients under supervision. They are also
required to complete studies in Integrated Clinical Communication Skills and bioethics.
At the end of the year, they sit for Part II of the Third Professional
Examination in Medicine and Surgery. Besides the essential medical knowledge and skills, the curriculum
puts equal emphasis on medical professionalism, bioethics and communication
skills. These topics are covered through interactive teaching and learning
across all years of the programme. Students learn a wide range of biomedical knowledge and its
clinical application in the first three years. They have clinical contact
including structured practical sessions in the clinical skills laboratory and
clinical rotations to various disciplines. In the clinical years, students
acquire clinical competence through participation in the work of clinical
teams in delivering day-to-day care of patients. Medical students are required to pass all the Professional
Examinations and periodic assessments of individual courses and modules for conferment
of the MBChB Degrees. Graduates are required to complete one-year of
internship at recognized hospitals before they are qualified to apply for
registration as a medical doctor in Hong Kong.
Elective Students may opt to enroll in Elective and spend an intercalated
year in disaster relief, humanitarian work, extended overseas internship or
academic training in clinician-scientific research. These students are
required to fulfill the assessment requirements as indicated in the course descriptions. Medicine Programme (MBChB) Global Physician-Leadership Stream
(GPS) All GPS students have to complete the study scheme prescribed
above and the following courses: (1) GPS students admitted in Medicine Year One or Medicine Year
Two are required to complete satisfactorily GPS Leadership Workshop Series I
(GPSU2000), GPS Leadership Workshop Series IIA (GPSU3000), GPS Leadership
Workshop Series IIB (GPSU4000) and GPS Leadership Workshop Series III
(GPSU4200). GPS students admitted in Medicine Year Three are required to
complete satisfactorily GPS Leadership Workshop Series IIB (GPSU4000) and GPS
Leadership Workshop Series III (GPSU4200). (2) Under close supervision of a mentor, GPS students must
complete a GPS project within the period from Medicine Year Three until
Medicine Year Five. These students are required to submit an end-of-project
report no later than the end of Medicine Year Five. A student can be
exempted from Selected Study Modules if s/he opts for submission of an
interim report of the GPS Project by the end of Medicine Year Four. (3) GPS students are required to take GPS Internship (a 4 to
6-week duration internship attachment in a local or an overseas institution).
GPS Internship is arranged at the latest by the time of Medicine Year Five.
GPS students who fulfill the requirement of GPS Internship can be exempted
from the course requirement of Clinical Elective in Medicine Year Five. Explanatory Notes: 1.  |  Students who have completed all the courses listed below are
eligible to apply for exemption of 3 units of University Core English
Language Requirement course ELTU2016:
(i)  |  MEDF1120 Public Health;
(ii)  |  MEDU2500 Doctor and Patient I;
(iii)  |  MEDU3500 Doctor and Patient II;
(iv)  |  SSMU3000 Selected Study Modules;
(v)  |  MEDU4610 Senior Medical Clerkship; and
(vi)  |  MEDU4710 Senior Surgical Dressership.
2.  |  Courses taken in bioethics that are offered by the CUHK Centre
for Bioethics are considered as alternatives to fulfilling 5 units of the
University General Education courses in Areas B, C and D.
3.  |  Students admitted with qualifications listed below are eligible
to apply for exemptions in MEDU1110 Systemic Anatomy (2 units) and MEDU1130
Cells and Tissues (2 units):
(i)  |  attained a total score of at least 44 in 7 HKDSE subjects,
including at least 5* in English, Biology and Chemistry; or
(ii)  |  admitted to the MBChB Programme on the strength of GCE Advanced
Level (AL)/International Advanced Level (IAL) having obtained:
(a)  |  Grade A* in four AL/IAL subjects with overall average of 95% or
above; and
(b)  |  Grade A* in Biology (AL/IAL) and Chemistry (AL/IAL); or
(iii)  |  admitted to the MBChB Programme on the strength of IB having
obtained:
(a)  |  total IB score of 43 or above out of 45; and
(b)  |  at least Grade 6 in Biology (HL) and Chemistry (HL), of which
either one must be Grade 7; or
(iv)  |  admitted to the MBChB Programme on the strength of Cambridge
Pre-U having obtained:
(a)  |  Grade D2 or above in four subjects (which may be substituted by
Grade A* in GCE AL/IAL); and
(b)  |  Grade D2 or above in Biology and Chemistry (which may be
substituted by Grade A* in GCE AL/IAL); or
(v)  |  passed relevant Chemistry and Biology courses at undergraduate
degree level, that are approved by the Faculty, encompassing students who:
(a)  |  have already obtained a bachelor’s (or higher) degree in a
science (and related) discipline at a recognized university; or
(b)  |  have successfully completed a study programme of at least two
years’ duration at a medical school whose curriculum is similar and
compatible to that of CUHK; or
(c)  |  have successfully completed a study programme of at least one
year’s duration at a medical school whose curriculum is similar and
compatible to that of CUHK; or
(d)  |  are still in their undergraduate studies.
4.  |  Students are required to apply for the exemptions. When
exemption from a particular course is recognized, students can only be
exempted from the course but not the units.
5.  |  The requirements of students with a normative study period different
from those prescribed above have to be approved by the Board of the Faculty
of Medicine on a case-by-case basis.
